text.title: Agent Postmortem Template Core Rules
text.summary: Hard rules every agent-as-defendant postmortem must satisfy. These fields replace the generic incident template — omitting any one collapses the layer-attribution discipline this methodology exists to enforce.

rule.statement: Every postmortem records the exact model identifier (provider plus version pin, e.g. anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929) and the deployment timestamp. "Claude" or "GPT-4" is not a model identifier and fails the template gate.
rule.rationale: Half of agent regressions are silent model upgrades. Without a pinned version string the postmortem cannot distinguish a prompt bug from a provider-side model swap.

rule.statement: Every postmortem includes a prompt diff: the system prompt at the time of incident vs. the system prompt at the last green eval run, rendered with all variables substituted. Diff is byte-exact, not paraphrased.
rule.rationale: Prompt regressions are the #1 cause of agent incidents and are invisible without a rendered byte-diff. Paraphrased diffs mask conditional branches that fired only in the failing context.

rule.statement: The Layer Attribution section assigns the root cause to exactly one of six layers: model, system prompt, tool schema, tool result, retrieved context, user input. If the team cannot pick one, the postmortem is incomplete — escalate to a second reviewer, do not close.
rule.rationale: "Combination of factors" is the agent-postmortem version of "human error" — it forecloses corrective action. Forcing a single primary layer routes the fix to the owner who can act.

rule.statement: Every postmortem records an eval delta: score on the incident's regression case before the fix, and a target score after. Closure requires the after-fix eval to be green; merging the fix without re-running the eval is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without eval delta the fix is theatre — the team learns nothing about whether the change generalised. Eval-gated closure is the only way to prevent the same hallucination class re-shipping next sprint.

rule.statement: Replay artefacts (rendered prompt, tool-call payloads, tool results, retrieved chunks, response) are attached to the postmortem as files or trace links. If a layer's artefact cannot be replayed, the postmortem records a Trace Gap with an explicit instrumentation action item.
rule.rationale: Replay is the difference between a postmortem and a story. Trace gaps are themselves valuable findings — they force instrumentation investment before the next incident.

example.code: Model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 (pinned in prod since 2026-04-12) Layer attributed: tool schema (description field missing units → model assumed seconds, API expected ms) Eval delta: regression case "tool_time_unit_conversion" 0/10 → 10/10 after schema fix Trace: trace_id=abc123, replay attached at incidents/2026-05-20-agent-postmortem.zip
Model: Claude Cause: model hallucinated Fix: improved prompt
example.note: No version pin, no layer attribution, no eval delta, no replay — the team will ship the same incident next month.
